title: Innovation Accounting

content.statement: Track startup progress with metrics that reveal whether the business model is working.
content.question: Are my metrics actionable cohort signals, or vanity numbers that hide stagnation?
content.equation: Innovation Score = Cohort Behavior × Conversion × Retention
content.mental_model: Startup-specific dashboard, different from accounting for stable companies.
content.business_lens: Track per-cohort activation, conversion, and retention; pivot when these stop improving.
content.life_lens: Pick measures that show whether your effort actually produces the outcome you want.
sop: Define actionable metrics, not vanity ones
Track each cohort's activation, conversion, retention
Set milestones that signal real progress
content.explainer: A startup with rising downloads but flat retention may look healthy and be dying. Innovation accounting forces honest assessment: is the engine actually pulling? Cohort analysis is the standard tool because it reveals trends time-series totals hide.
questions: Are my metrics actionable cohort signals, or vanity numbers that hide stagnation?
If you pulled up your dashboard right now, could you name the one number that would have to move before you changed a decision?
Which cohort, this month's or three months ago, actually retains better, and have you ever compared them side by side instead of looking at totals?

definitions.def: A group of customers who started in the same period.
The moment a new user reaches their first meaningful win in a product.
The percentage of customers who keep paying or using a product over time.

content.equation_example: Cohort behavior 60% engaged, conversion 20%, retention 45%. 0.60 times 0.20 times 0.45 equals an Innovation Score of about 5.4%. Higher/rising means your engine is actually pulling, engaged users convert and stick; lower/falling means growth numbers are hiding a leaky core.
content.closing_statement: Rising engagement means little if it never converts.

caption:
Principle Nº 290: Innovation Accounting

Track startup progress with metrics that reveal whether the business model is working. A startup with rising downloads but flat retention may look healthy and be dying.

Ask yourself: Are my metrics actionable cohort signals, or vanity numbers that hide stagnation?
